eyebrow: Brand Palette · Sourced from real identities
tagline: Seven real brands, read for their actual color — shipped products and researched public identities alike.
metaNote:
Color sourced from real,
shipped brand identities
sourcing: Four of these are the nonoun studio's own live products, sampled straight from their real, currently-shipped design-system exports (Neutral, custom status colors, and all). The other three are researched from each brand's own public identity — Corsa from its own in-app color system and brand mark, Nike and Burger King from their published guidelines and known brand assets.

volumes.title: Real identities
volumes.eyebrow: Brands · Vol I · Sourced from real identities
volumes.h1: Seven real brands, read for their actual color.
preface: Every other category in this gallery samples color from a place, a material, a dish, a film — something to look at and read off. This one samples it from brands: four of the nonoun studio's own shipped products, plus three researched public identities. The four owned presets carry their REAL Neutral and status colors, exactly as shipped — not the generic derived-and-shared set every sourced/decorative preset in this gallery otherwise gets.

palettes.kicker: Maison · The product's own design system
palettes.title: A vivid indigo-violet system warmed by gold and deepened by magenta
palettes.source: Color from the product's own live design system, exported as-shipped: a vivid blue-violet primary, a warm gold-green secondary, and a deep magenta-violet tertiary.

d.text: Vivid indigo-violet leads the whole system.

s.text: Warm gold-green and deep magenta-violet carry the rest.

palettes.refuses: A soft pastel SaaS palette. Maison's indigo runs vivid and near-peak saturated, not muted.

palettes.kicker: Adia · The product's own design system
palettes.title: A cool sky-blue system deepened by indigo-violet
palettes.source: Color from the product's own live design system, exported as-shipped: a vivid sky/cyan primary, a pale aqua secondary, and a deep indigo-violet tertiary.

s.text: Pale aqua and deep indigo-violet round it out.

palettes.refuses: A warm, high-chroma palette. Adia stays cool by design — sky blue and quiet aqua, not heat.

palettes.kicker: BZZR · The product's own design system
palettes.title: An electric indigo-blue system charged by neon mint
palettes.source: Color from the product's own live design system, exported as-shipped: an extremely vivid electric-blue primary and a neon-mint secondary, both near-peak chroma.

d.text: Electric indigo-blue at near-maximum chroma leads.

s.text: Neon mint and a muted violet carry the charge.

palettes.refuses: A calm, desaturated palette. BZZR runs hot and electric by design — near-peak chroma on both its primary and secondary.

palettes.title: A modal-jazz mood of deep blue, midnight teal, brass, and smoke
palettes.source: Color from the modal session: midnight blue, teal shadow, brass gold, smoke grey, and a warm spotlight.

d.text: Midnight blue leads the modal mood.

s.text: Teal shadow, brass gold, and smoke grey.

a.text: Amber spot and bass wood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded sepia. Modal jazz is deep blue and teal warmed by brass — a cool-blue mood, not a brown nostalgia.

palettes.name: primary
palettes.description: The cool modal mood.

palettes.name: primary-muted
palettes.description: The cool club shadow.

palettes.name: secondary
palettes.description: The horn under the light.

palettes.name: secondary-muted
palettes.description: The hazy air.

palettes.name: accent
palettes.description: The single warm light.

palettes.name: accent-muted
palettes.description: The double-bass body.

palettes.kicker: Corsa · Grand Tour cycling · Italy · France · Spain
palettes.title: A loud, graphic Grand-Tour identity — Giro rosa and Tour yellow over road blue, punctuated by the red, green, purple and blue classification jerseys
palettes.source: Color from the three Grand Tours, read for their real jersey and newsprint origins: the Giro's maglia rosa (pink because La Gazzetta dello Sport is printed on pink paper) and the Tour's maillot jaune (≈Pantone 123 C, yellow because L'Auto was printed on yellow paper), over a road blue and a muted lagoon; the Vuelta's maillot rojo, a limestone-and-cream stone ground, and the classification jerseys — Giro azzurra (blue, mountains) and ciclamino (cyclamen purple, points), Tour maillot vert (green, points) — over gravel, redwood-brown and pine terrain neutrals. The palette of a between-the-wars-to-Moser-era cycling poster, not a faded photograph.

d.text: Giro rosa leads as the identity's loud signature, over a cool cobble-grey ground.

s.text: Road blue, Tour yellow, aged newsprint, muted lagoon and limestone cream carry the working band.

a.text: Vuelta red and the azzurra, vert and ciclamino classification jerseys as graphic punctuation.

palettes.refuses: A muted, faded-photograph nostalgia. The Grand Tour's heritage is loud and graphic — maglia-rosa pink, maillot-jaune yellow, and gas-station-signage jersey reds and greens, printed on pink and yellow newsprint, not sepia.

palettes.name: neutral
palettes.description: Cool pavé cobblestone grey — the road under every tour.

palettes.name: primary
palettes.description: The maglia rosa — a vivid poster pink from La Gazzetta's pink pages.

palettes.name: primary-muted
palettes.description: The soft, warm Gazzetta pink-paper tint beside the vivid rosa.

palettes.name: secondary
palettes.description: The tarmac-and-signage blue of the race route.

palettes.name: secondary-muted
palettes.description: A desaturated Venetian-lagoon blue-grey beside the road blue.

palettes.name: tertiary
palettes.description: The maillot rojo — the Vuelta leader jersey, red since 2010.

palettes.name: tertiary-muted
palettes.description: Warm pale stone and paper — the poster stock.

palettes.name: quartery
palettes.description: The maillot jaune, ≈Pantone 123 C — golden, from L'Auto's yellow newsprint.

palettes.name: quartery-muted
palettes.description: Aged L'Auto newsprint — a muted mustard behind the maillot jaune.

palettes.name: accent-1
palettes.description: The Giro mountains jersey — blue since 2012 (Banca Mediolanum).

palettes.name: accent-2
palettes.description: The Tour points jersey — a bright grass green.

palettes.name: accent-3
palettes.description: The Giro points jersey — cyclamen, an intense purple-magenta.

palettes.name: accent-4
palettes.description: Strade-bianche gravel — a warm mid brown.

palettes.name: accent-5
palettes.description: A deep reddish heartwood brown.

palettes.name: accent-6
palettes.description: A deep pine/forest green (read from 'Redwood green').

palettes.title: Jet black and box white, held to near-monochrome, lit by one loud swoosh orange.
palettes.source: Nike's codified identity has been black and white since 1971 — the Swoosh in PMS Black 6 C, floating in negative space — with the shoebox and Nike+ orange (≈Pantone 1655 C) and the deep University Red of team kit as the only loud reads.

swatches.name: Jet black, PMS Black 6 C
swatches.note: The Swoosh and wordmark ink — a blue-black, not flat black.

swatches.name: Box white
swatches.note: The negative space the Swoosh floats in.

swatches.name: Sail, warm bone
swatches.note: The off-white of leather and canvas uppers.

swatches.name: Total Orange, swoosh orange
swatches.note: The shoebox and Nike+ accent, ≈Pantone 1655 C.

swatches.name: University Red
swatches.note: The deep swoosh red of team kit.

d.text: Jet black is the mark — the Swoosh, the wordmark, the ink.

s.text: Box white, technical grey, and sail carry the ground.

a.text: Swoosh orange and University Red as small, loud reads.

palettes.refuses: Not a rainbow of past colorway drops — Nike's brand identity is disciplined black and white, lit by a single orange-red accent, never a full spectrum.

palettes.kicker: Burger King · The Flame Identity · 2021 rebrand
palettes.title: A flame-grill on a bun: fiery red over char-brown and mayo cream, lit by melty yellow and a rare crunchy green.
palettes.source: Sampled straight from Burger King's 2021 flame identity (in-house team with JKR). The six chips are the guideline swatches themselves — Fiery Red, Flaming Orange, BBQ Brown, Mayo Egg White, Melty Yellow and Crunchy Green — an analogous, appetite-warm system drawn from the Whopper's real ingredients and the trademark flame-grill.

swatches.name: Fiery Red
swatches.note: The wordmark bun; the brand's primary, appetite-driving red.

swatches.name: Flaming Orange
swatches.note: The flame-grill glow that warms the packaging.

swatches.name: BBQ Brown
swatches.note: The char-grilled sear that anchors the whole identity.

swatches.name: Mayo Egg White
swatches.note: The warm cream ground that carries the packaging.

swatches.name: Melty Yellow
swatches.note: Molten cheese; the loud secondary highlight.

swatches.name: Crunchy Green
swatches.note: Fresh lettuce; the small, rare fresh-ingredient accent.

d.text: Fiery Red is the brand's body — the primary and the signature.

s.text: Flaming Orange, BBQ Brown, and mayo cream as the warm working band.

a.text: Melty Yellow and a rare crunchy green as small, loud reads.

palettes.refuses: Not the 1990s photoreal, blue-swooshed fast-food logo it replaced — the flame identity is warm and fully analogous, unashamedly appetite-red, with no cool blue anywhere in it.
